items.title: Retail investing has surged in India, but rural participation remains low, says Sebi Chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey
items.summary: India's securities markets have seen substantial growth.  Retail investor numbers have surged, and market capitalization has climbed significantly.  However, actual investment participation lags behind awareness.  SEBI is enhancing investor protection and launching initiatives to boost financial literacy.  Disciplined long-term investing is encouraged for financial empowerment.

items.title: How Trump's crypto venture and Iran's top exchange tapped into the same industry networks
items.summary: Sun and ‌Binance, the crypto exchange owned by Zhao, are both prominent backers of World Liberty Financial, the crypto firm co-founded by Trump and his family

items.title: India Inc's earnings growth to slow down in next 12-18 months, warns Moody’s Ratings. Here’s why
items.summary: Moody’s Ratings warns that India’s corporate earnings growth may slow over the next 12–18 months due to rising input costs, rupee depreciation, supply-chain disruptions, and labour market uncertainty. The agency also flagged weaker consumption, delayed investments, and sectoral pressures across autos, airlines, metals, and oil marketing companies amid global risks.

items.title: BEE showcases India’s low-energy housing model in Baku amid energy conservation push
items.summary: The initiative reflects the government’s effort to treat energy efficiency as an economic strategy rather than a stand-alone environmental programme

items.title: FPIs sell over  ₹2 lakh crore in Indian stock market so far in 2026. Are they driving the boom-and-bust cycle?
items.summary: In 2026, FPIs have aggressively sold over  ₹2.19 lakh crore in Indian equities, surpassing 2025 outflows. This has weakened the rupee significantly, with total FPI ownership now at 15%. Domestic investors are absorbing some pressure, but concerns over global capital flight persist.

items.title: Airtel dethrones HDFC Bank as India’s second-most valuable company
items.summary: Airtel’s total market cap stood at ₹11,80,328 crore, narrowly edging past HDFC Bank’s ₹11,82,223 crore — a gap that fluctuated through the session after Airtel hit an intraday high of ₹1,953.80.

items.title: Anarock Group FY26 revenue rises 25% to Rs 946 cr realty news
items.summary: Anarock Group, one of the leading property consultants in the country, posted the revenue growth across its core residential, office space, investment advisory and retail verticals, he highlighted.

items.title: NTPC Group's installed electricity generation capacity crosses 90GW mark
items.summary: State-owned NTPC has crossed a significant milestone, achieving over 90 GW of installed electricity generation capacity. This expansion was driven by the successful trial operation of Unit 2 at its Patratu Vidyut Utpadan Nigam arm. The company is actively pursuing renewable energy targets and diversifying its energy portfolio.

items.title: Small-cap stock under  ₹50: Promoter-backed DII holds stake in tech stock in Q4FY26 despite selloff on D-Street
items.summary: On Monday, Kellton Tech share price fell by over 3% on NSE, touching an intraday low of  ₹14.49 apiece. According to data available on NSE, Matnic Finvest has maintained its stake at 32.97% in the March quarter (Q4FY26).

items.title: Liberty Mutual raises India stake to 74% in Liberty General Insurance
items.summary: The remaining 26% stake in LGI is held by Enam Securities

items.title: MCX to facilitate delivery of India refined silver
items.summary: Move comes on the back of Government decision to curb import of gold and silver to bridge the current account deficit.

items.title: Hind Rectifiers shares slide 3.5% as strong FY26 revenue masks margin pressure
items.summary: The stock touched an intraday low of ₹797.55 before recovering, and trades at a P/E of 84.28 — well above the broader market.

items.title: EPACK Prefab posts 34.5% revenue jump in FY26, stock falls 4%
items.summary: The stock traded at ₹192.41 on the NSE, down 4% from ₹200.42, with sell orders making up over 80% of the total traded quantity

items.title: Income Tax Rules 2026: Form 16 replaced, PAN-TDS rules changed; 8 key changes to know
items.summary: Income Tax Rules 2026: The government has introduced major changes in income tax forms under the new Income Tax Rules 2026, including the replacement of Form 16 with Form 130 and revised PAN application forms. Several TDS and tax audit forms have also been merged or renamed to simplify tax compliance for FY 2025-26.

items.title: Coforge to Tech Mahindra: Tech stocks extend rally for second session; Nifty IT jumps over 2%
items.summary: Coforge, Tech Mahindra, and other tech stocks rallied on May 18, with the NIFTY IT index rising 2.4%. Analysts attribute this to currency weakness and export earnings, despite concerns over AI disruption and mixed earnings guidance from major firms, leading to a decline of 25.3% in 2026.

items.title: FIIs trim holdings in 10 midcap stocks over two quarters; shares drop up to 50%
items.summary: FII selling, midcap stocks, falling FII stake, foreign investor exit, stock market trends, Kaynes Technology, KPIT Technologies, Thermax shares, Bandhan Bank, midcap selloff

items.title: GMR Hyderabad International Airport commissions cargo terminal 2
items.summary: Spread across an area of 16864 sq. m, the new terminal has been engineered for operational efficiency and long-term scalability

items.title: German 10-year bond yield stays at 15-year high amid global bond rout
items.summary: Global bond markets are experiencing a selloff. Investors are concerned about rising energy prices due to the Middle East conflict. This could fuel inflation and lead to further interest rate hikes. German 10-year bond yields have reached a 15-year high. Italian 10-year yields are also up. European Central Bank head Christine Lagarde acknowledged concerns.

items.title: NTPC Group total installed capacity surpasses 90 GW
items.summary: The milestone was achieved following the successful completion of trial operations of Unit 2 (800 megawatt) of Patratu Vidyut Utpadan Nigam (PVUNL), a subsidiary project comprising 3x800 MW units

items.title: Oswal Pumps shares slide, posted record FY26 earnings
items.summary: The stock, listed in June 2025, has lost roughly 29% year-to-date and trades at less than half its 52-week high of ₹888.40 hit in August 2025

items.title: Ford no longer looks like an also-ran in the global EV race—it’s just that it has taken an alternate route
items.summary: Ford’s electric vehicle ambitions may have spluttered so far, but its new energy business could keep the carmaker in play. It is betting heavily on grid batteries that could serve multiple purposes—and keep its electric mobility ambitions alive.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit 2026: Decoding the future of Indian stock market in uncertain times
items.summary: Indian investors navigate a complex wealth creation landscape. Geopolitical shifts and rapid AI adoption demand new strategies. The ET Alpha Wealth Summit 2026 on June 4 in Mumbai will gather top minds. Discussions will focus on AI, private markets, and global capital flows. Learn how to build resilient portfolios and identify future opportunities.

items.title: Table Space adds 5 lakh sq ft in Hyderabad amid rising GCC demand
items.summary: Table Space has significantly expanded its Hyderabad presence by adding nearly 500,000 square feet of workspace. This growth is fueled by increasing demand from global capability centers and various enterprises. The company's largest deal is at Grava Business Park in Kokapet. This expansion strengthens Hyderabad's position as a key office and GCC hub in India.

items.title: Commodity Radar: Rupee's record lows support gold prices. Can fresh base formation trigger upside?
items.summary: Gold futures on MCX are holding above key support levels as rupee weakness nears record lows, boosting domestic prices despite global softness. Analysts say MCX gold is showing early signs of base formation, with consolidation above Rs 1.58 lakh potentially paving the way for further upside if momentum sustains.

items.title: Zydus Wellness FY26 profit falls by 47% to ₹197 crore despite revenue growth
items.summary: The board has recommended a final dividend of ₹1.20 per equity share of face value ₹2, subject to shareholder approval at the AGM scheduled for August 4

items.title: Gold loan giant Muthoot FinCorp plans Rs 4,000 crore IPO. Check details
items.summary: Muthoot FinCorp has approved plans to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore via an IPO, aiming to fund expansion in the fast-growing gold loan market. The company, fully owned by its promoter family, plans a fresh issue of shares as strong gold prices and demand boost sector growth prospects.

items.title: Bank of India raises fixed deposit rates for medium- and long-term tenures
items.summary: Senior Citizens will receive an additional 0.50% p.a. over the applicable rate for deposits maturing between six months and less than three years, while Super Senior Citizens will get an extra 0.65% p.a. for the same tenure band

items.title: Amazon Now to deploy 1,000 Eicher electric trucks
items.summary: These vehicles will move goods between Amazon's fulfilment hubs, typically located in the outskirts of the city and Amazon Now micro-fulfilment centres located closer to customers

items.title: Indian bond yields spike to six-week high as crude oil price rally jolts markets
items.summary: Bond markets from Tokyo to New York came under pressure following fresh attacks in the Gulf region. The yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note surged to a 15-month high of 4.6310%.

items.title: Gold futures rise ₹598 in volatile trade amid tensions in West Asia
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, the yellow metal for the June delivery appreciated by ₹598, or 0.38% to ₹1,59,145 per 10 grams

items.title: India's hotel sector attracts USD 185 mn investment in Jan-Mar, jumps 58 pc: JLL
items.summary: Investments in India's hotel sector saw a significant jump. In the first quarter of 2026, investments rose 58 percent to USD 185 million. This reflects strong investor confidence in the hotel business's growth potential. Last year, investments had already surged 67 percent. This upward trend continues, driven by ample liquidity and new opportunities.

items.title: From Hollywood to Latin Cinema: Film Master Media Distribution LLC celebrated at Abu Dhabi Film Festival
items.summary: Film Master Media wins Abu Dhabi Film Festival award for global distribution, expanding diverse international cinema reach across the Middle East.

items.title: Strong Q4 rural recovery runs into rising cost and monsoon risks
items.summary: Consumption-linked companies posted their strongest sales growth in years in Q4FY26. Analysts now warn that crude-led inflation and monsoon risks could choke demand in H1FY27.

items.title: JSW Energy sells 2.5 crore JSW Steel shares for Rs 3,150 cr via bulk deal
items.summary: JSW Energy on Monday said it sold 2.5 crore equity shares in JSW Steel through a bulk deal on the NSE, raising Rs 3,150 crore. Following the transaction, JSW Energy’s remaining holding in JSW Steel stands at 4.5 crore equity shares, according to a regulatory filing.

items.title: Medanta expects Noida hospital to turn cash positive within months as expansion drive accelerates
items.summary: Medanta's new Noida hospital is projected to become cash positive within three to four months, with its startup losses being the primary reason for a recent margin dip. Established facilities are performing strongly, and the company plans significant brownfield bed expansion alongside new greenfield projects, aiming for a consolidated EBITDA margin of 24-26%.

items.title: Jute industry seeks new West Bengal government’s intervention to tide over raw material crisis
items.summary: At least 14 mills across the Hooghly industrial belt have either suspended operations or are facing severe production disruptions, according to IJMA sources

items.title: Old shopping funnel is gone: Gen Z is rewriting rules of retail
items.summary: Gen Z is rapidly reshaping India’s online retail market, forcing brands and platforms to rethink traditional marketing strategies. Nearly half of India’s online fashion and beauty shoppers now belong to Gen Z, with companies like Amazon, Nykaa, Myntra and Shein tailoring their offerings to meet the cohort’s fast-changing preferences and high engagement levels.

items.title: Tata Steel’s strong quarter meets a Dutch roadblock
items.summary: Shares fall 4% despite solid Q4FY26 as possible closure of coke and gas plants in the Netherlands raises margin risks; pricing gains and volume growth offer partial cushion.

items.title: Silver futures slide to ₹2.7 lakh/kg as oil rally fuels inflation concerns
items.summary: On MCX, the white metal for July delivery depreciated by ₹1,832, or nearly 1% to ₹2,70,054 per kilogram, extending losses for the third straight session.

items.title: ICICI Pru Life shares crash nearly 9% after Prudential plans stake cut below 10%
items.summary: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance shares fell sharply on Monday after reports that promoter Prudential plc may reduce its stake below 10% following the acquisition of Bharti Life Insurance. The move raised concerns over ownership structure and strategic alignment, though ICICI Bank clarified it will retain majority control in the insurer.

items.title: Why gold weakens below ₹1,58,500 amid Fed rate hike fears
items.summary: The sell-off was triggered by hotter-than-expected US inflation data

items.title: Amazon Now to deploy 1,000 Eicher electric trucks
items.summary: Amazon India is introducing 1,000 electric trucks for its Amazon Now service by 2028. Fifty trucks are already on the road. These vehicles will transport goods between fulfillment centers and micro-fulfillment centers. The initiative aims to support quick commerce operations across India, starting with key cities.

items.title: JSW Energy sells 2.5 crore JSW Steel shares for ₹3,150 cr via bulk deal
items.summary: Post-transaction, the company's balance holding is 4,50,38,350 equity shares of JSW Steel Ltd, a regulatory filing said.

items.title: Tata Power, Druk Green ink pact to build training framework for clean energy in Bhutan
items.summary: Training programmes will be delivered through Tata Power Skill Development Institute

items.title: China agrees to boost trade for U.S. agricultural products following Trump-Xi summit
items.summary: The announcement came two days after President Donald Trump returned from a high-stakes summit in Beijing where he sought to ease the impact on American farmers from the trade war he launched last year

items.title: FCI to raise ₹50,000 crore short-term loan for foodgrain operations
items.summary: Tenders submitted in this regard will be opened on May 22; Total borrowings under the tender will not exceed ₹75,000 crore

items.title: TCS asks managers to classify 5% staff as underperformers
items.summary: Internal emails show TCS pushing managers to place 5% of employees in the lowest performance band, stoking fears of further workforce cuts after last year’s retrenchment exercise.

items.title: Platinum demand for Indian vehicle production up 1% in Q1, but jewellery offtake down 15%
items.summary: World Platinum Investment Council expects the global market to be in deficit to the tune of 297,000 ounces in 2026, despite surprlus production in the first quarter

items.title: Nifty Bank falls 500 points as PNB, Canara Bank, SBI, other stocks decline up to 3%. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Indian bank stocks tumbled, dragging the Nifty Bank index down as a weakening rupee and rising global bond yields soured market sentiment. Several major banks saw significant declines, with analysts pointing to key technical levels. The rupee hit an all-time low against the dollar, exacerbating concerns for the financial sector.

items.title: EXIM Bank eyes 10% jump in loan book in FY27 despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: The FY27 loan growth number is lower than the 12 per cent growth it achieved in FY26, but currency fluctuation had a significant role in lifting the number.

items.title: India can ride out disruption after Russian oil waiver lapses
items.summary: India's oil refiners are prepared for changes in Russian crude supply. Weaker domestic demand and available oil from the US and Middle East offer alternatives. China's reduced market presence also helps maintain supply. Maintenance at major refiners has increased Russian crude in storage. This provides Indian refiners with more options in the short term.

items.title: India must aim higher: cost optimization must not define the country’s tryst with artificial intelligence
items.summary: Indian firms have scant incentive to invest in foundational research for artificial intelligence (AI). But the cost of not doing so may be even higher. Indian firms should look beyond quarterly profit-and-loss statements for a longer-term view.

items.title: India directs State-run banks, insurance firms to cut costs, shift to EVs
items.summary:
The order, part of a broader austerity push, will cover institutions like the ‌State Bank of India, Bank of
Baroda and Life Insurance Corp of India ‌and million of their employees across the ‌country

items.title: NSE EGR trading goes live. How can gold investors buy, sell and invest?
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has launched live trading in its Electronic Gold Receipts (EGR) segment, enabling investors to trade gold digitally via demat accounts. Backed by physical gold stored in regulated vaults, EGRs allow flexible denominations and exchange-based trading. The initiative aims to deepen India’s bullion market and improve transparency in gold pricing.

items.title: Govt weighs options to revive stalled IDBI Bank privatisation
items.summary: One of the options is to reduce the reserve price by as much as 20% after buyers balked in the previous round, which was halted in March, sources said

items.title: US-Iran war: Why PM Modi visited UAE amid Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz crisis
items.summary: Amid escalating tensions from the US-Iran war and soaring oil prices, India's Prime Minister Modi's recent visit to the UAE aims to secure energy supplies and foster deeper economic ties. With the UAE's strategic shift in oil policy, India seeks to diversify imports and enhance energy security.

items.title: El Nino spells trouble for kharif harvests across India
items.summary: A study by scientists at an ICAR institute has found that El Niño events have consistently reduced yields of major kharif crops in India, with significant impacts across multiple states. Research led by Subash N Pillai at the ICAR-Indian Institute of Farming Systems Research shows that paddy output fell by over 10% in 77 districts, while maize production declined similarly in 65 districts during El Niño years.

items.title: Why Apollo Tyres’ strong quarter may not mark a turning point
items.summary: The management indicated that input costs could rise in the high-teens in Q1FY27, driven primarily by elevated natural rubber prices. Further, new vehicle purchases would be impacted if fuel/commodity costs continue their inflationary trajectory.

items.title: Up to 40% NCR buyers prefer homes with dedicated workspaces as WFH reshapes buying behaviour: Experts
items.summary: Narendra Modi has urged citizens to conserve fuel, avoid non-essential spending, and work from home where possible amid rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia. While aimed at tackling short-term economic pressures, the appeal is also accelerating an ongoing shift in India’s housing market, as remote work continues to influence home-buying preferences and living patterns.

items.title: 5 investing mistakes that you should avoid during volatile markets
items.summary: Speaking on Zee Business, the financial experts, Hemant Rustagi, CEO of Wiseinvest and mutual fund expert Vishwajeet Parashar, highlighted 5 common investing mistakes that investors should avoid, especially during volatile market phases.

items.title: Tata Power, Druk Green ink pact to build training framework for clean energy in Bhutan
items.summary: Tata Power has signed an initial agreement with Druk Green Power Corporation to create a training framework for developing 5,000 MW of clean energy capacity in Bhutan.

items.title: Jana Small Finance Bank to raise Rs 728 crore via preferential issue; TVS-linked fund among key investors
items.summary: Bank approves Rs 728.5 crore preferential warrant issue and plans additional Rs 500 crore Tier-II fundraising through NCDs.

items.title: Goldman says central banks want more gold for their reserves
items.summary: In a bid to bolster reserves, central banks are poised to increase their gold purchases, potentially reviving gold prices as we approach the year's conclusion. Goldman Sachs forecasts a marked rise in these acquisitions, driven by geopolitical uncertainties that are pushing central banks to rethink their asset portfolios.

items.title: EXIM Bank eyes 10% jump in loan book in FY27 despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: Export-Import Bank of India expects its loan book to grow 10% in FY27 despite geopolitical tensions in West Asia, according to Managing Director and CEO Harsha Bangari. The projected growth is lower than FY26’s 12% rise, which was partly boosted by favourable currency movements as a large share of the bank’s loans are denominated in foreign currencies.

items.title: Why is Vodafone Idea share price falling despite a surprise  ₹51,970-crore profit in Q4? Explained
items.summary: Vodafone Idea share price opened at  ₹12.81 today as against the last closing price of  ₹12.96 and dropped to an intraday low of  ₹12.45 soon thereafter, shedding 3.93%. The company had posted its Q4 results on Saturday.

items.title: AI, not engineers for coding: Salesforce to spend $300 million on Anthropic tokens, CEO Marc Benioff says
items.summary: Benioff's plans, coupled with his previous announcement that Salesforce will not hire any more engineers in 2025 due to productivity gains from AI, signal a decisive shift — the automation of writing code.

items.title: Market moves driven more by psychology than fundamentals: Samir Arora
items.summary: Indian stock markets are caught between global worries and strong domestic company profits. Foreign investor activity, oil price swings, and economic news are shaping market mood. Samir Arora of Helios Capital believes current market jitters are more about perception than lasting damage. He notes corporate earnings are exceeding expectations.

items.title: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO Day 1: Issue booked 20% so far. Check GMP, subscription status, key details to know
items.summary: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO opened on 18 May, priced between  ₹52 to  ₹55 per share. The company specialises in dry fruits, particularly cashew nuts, and aims to raise  ₹24.53 crore through this offering. Subscription status shows 20% on day 1.

items.title: Markets pare morning losses by afternoon; IT rallies while metal, power stocks stay under pressure
items.summary: As of 12.45 PM, the BSE Sensex was trading at 75,087.71, down 150.28 points or 0.20%, having recovered from the morning low of 74,447.73 — a near 640-point intraday recovery

items.title: TVS Venu, Shruti Lohia-promoted firms to buy minority stakes in Jana Small Finance Bank
items.summary: Jana Small Finance Bank is raising Rs 728 crore by selling equity shares, with Shruti Lohia's firm set to acquire around 4% and TVS Venu Group taking a 9.9% stake on a fully diluted basis. The bank is planning to upgrade to a universal bank, having reapplied for a license after an initial rejection.

items.title: Govt weighs options to revive stalled IDBI Bank privatization
items.summary: Indian authorities are exploring ways to revive the long-delayed sale of a majority stake in IDBI Bank after bids fell short of expectations earlier this year. Officials are considering lowering the reserve price by up to 20% and reducing reliance on the bank’s market price to make the deal more attractive to buyers, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: India-EU FTA to create new opportunities for industries, investors, innovators: Modi
items.summary: India holds tremendous potential for collaboration with Sweden in high-tech sectors such as AI, health-tech, green mobility

items.title: Amber Enterprises shares crash nearly 15% after Q4 results disappoint on PAT front
items.summary: The stock opened at ₹8,118, touched a high of ₹8,148.50, and slid to a low of ₹6,980 during the session

items.title: Bharti Airtel claims No.2 spot: How it beat HDFC Bank to become India's second most valuable company
items.summary: Bharti Airtel has overtaken HDFC Bank to become India’s second most valuable listed company. Airtel shares rose over 2%, taking its market capitalisation to around Rs 11.8 lakh crore, while HDFC Bank shares fell more than 2%, reducing its market cap to nearly Rs 11.7 lakh crore. Reliance Industries remains India’s most valuable company with a market capitalisation of about Rs 18 lakh crore.

items.title: US tech boom isn’t a dotcom bubble, but valuations warrant caution: Viram Shah of Vested Finance
items.summary: The Magnificent Seven now account for roughly 35% of the S&amp;P 500’s market capitalisation, the highest concentration in modern history. The CAPE ratio is close to 40 — near dotcom-era levels — while the Buffett Indicator stands at about 230% of GDP.

items.title: Raja Shivaji storms past Rs 114 crore to rewrite Marathi box office history
items.summary: Raja Shivaji, a historical drama produced by Jio Studios and Mumbai Film Company, has shattered box office records, becoming the highest-grossing Marathi film ever. Directed by Riteish Deshmukh, the film has garnered Rs 93.10 crore nett in India and Rs 114.80 crore worldwide gross, demonstrating strong audience appeal and a significant milestone for Marathi cinema.

items.title: L Catterton said to be in talks to acquire minority stake in Bengaluru-based Nandhana Palace
items.summary: Private equity firm L Catterton is in discussions to acquire a stake in Nandhana Palace, which is expanding its restaurant chain across India amidst increasing investor interest in the food services sector.

items.title: Finance Ministry asks PSU banks, insurers to adopt austerity measures, shift to EV
items.summary: India’s finance ministry has directed state-run banks, insurers and financial institutions to cut costs through measures such as limiting travel, holding meetings virtually and shifting to electric vehicles. The order applies to institutions including State Bank of India, Bank of Baroda and Life Insurance Corporation of India.

items.title: The hype era is over: Agricultural robotics must now prove cost-per-acre value
items.summary: Made in Bharat agricultural robotics has the chance to set a global benchmark, but only after it has earned that right at home

items.title: Balmer Lawrie shares tumble 6% after board says no to bonus issue, stock split & share buyback proposals
items.summary: Balmer Lawrie & Company's shares fell over 6% after the board decided against a proposed bonus issue, stock split, and share buyback, citing compliance with Ministry of Finance guidelines. The company did recommend a dividend of Rs 4.25 per share for FY26, while reporting a 12% year-on-year rise in Q4 net profit.

items.title: India fuel price hike fuels inflation pressure across sectors as crude risks rise
items.summary: As domestic petrol and diesel prices are hiked, analysts warn of broader cost pressures through logistics, FMCG, autos, and aviation, with outlook at risk if crude stays elevated

items.title: A reality check on govt.’s claim of 45 days of LPG ‘rolling stock’
items.summary: The Indian government’s claim of having 45 days’ worth of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) is met with skepticism as experts highlight discrepancies in storage capacity.

items.title: Plight of Sholavandan betel leaf farmers miserable as gale storms hit fields
items.summary: The plight of the farmers’ was miserable that most of them are facing ‘red’ and were unable to stop the rising losses due to multiple factors

items.title: Bitcoin price falls to two-week low amid growing US-Iran war worries. Where is it headed next?
items.summary: Bitcoin price: The world’s largest cryptocurrency dropped to as low as $76,711 on Monday — its weakest level since May 1 — before recovering part of the losses. Here's what experts say on the near-term outlook.

items.title: NSE launches live trading in Electronic Gold Receipts
items.summary: As part of the rollout, valuing and collection centres have already become operational in Ahmedabad and Mumbai

items.title: Maruti Suzuki commences second factory at Kharkhoda with 2.5-lakh units
items.summary: Once fully operational, the Kharkhoda facility will be among Suzuki’s largest four-wheeler manufacturing locations with capacity to produce one-million vehicles per annum. Its total capacity reaches 26.5 lakh units per annum now, including Gujarat plants

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Oracle Financial Services, Sun Pharma shares to buy
items.summary: Indian equity markets experienced a significant selloff on May 18, with Sensex and Nifty 50 dropping over 1%. The BSE Sensex fell over 1,000 points, erasing nearly  ₹9 lakh crore in investor wealth amid weak global cues and a bearish trend across sectors.

items.title: GE Aerospace to invest ₹100 crore in Pune manufacturing facility
items.summary: In three years, the aerospace major's investments in the facility, which makes critical components for commercial aircraft engines, total ₹510 crore

items.title: SBI Report: No direct impact of oil price hike on India's fiscal situation
items.summary: Fuel prices have risen by Rs 3 per litre. This move aims to reduce losses for oil companies. SBI's research report Ecowrap suggests that the impact on consumer inflation will be temporary. Annual fuel consumption is expected to remain stable. The government's fiscal situation is not directly affected by this price adjustment. State governments may see revenue changes if excise duties are altered.

items.title: Ajit Ranade: Why India’s central bank should not turn into a fiscal stabilizer for the government
items.summary: The Centre has been relying on the central bank’s surplus transfers to keep its fiscal deficit in check. This year’s transfer for 2025-26 may be even larger than last year’s record  ₹2.7 trillion payout. The danger is what this reliance says about RBI independence.

items.title: Jute industry seeks new Bengal govt's intervention to tide over raw material crisis
items.summary: West Bengal’s jute industry is staring at a major crisis as raw jute shortages and soaring prices disrupt operations across mills in the Hooghly industrial belt. Around 14 mills have either suspended work or cut production, while nearly 75,000 workers face job losses or irregular employment, industry bodies said.

items.title: ICICI Bank to retain majority stake in ICICI Pru Life as Prudential buys 75% in Bharti Life
items.summary: ICICI Bank will maintain its majority stake in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance following its partner Prudential's acquisition of a controlling stake in Bharti Life. This move necessitates Prudential reducing its holding in ICICI Prudential Life to below 10%. Despite market jitters, ICICI Bank reaffirms its long-term commitment to the life insurer.

items.title: Sikkim govt rolls out odd-even vehicle restriction norm to save fuel
items.summary: The implementation came after Prime Minister Narendra Modi appealed to people to reduce fuel consumption and spend more wisely in view of the West Asia crisis

items.title: Why Samsung shares just surged 7% to save Kospi from a tragic market meltdown
items.summary: Samsung Electronics shares surged sharply on Monday after the company resumed mediated wage negotiations with its largest labour union, easing fears of a disruptive strike. The rebound helped lift the KOSPI, as Samsung’s heavy index weight amplified the market impact. Investor sentiment improved after political and corporate leaders intervened to calm tensions.

items.title: Infosys, OFSS, TechM, other IT stocks gain up to 3% despite weak market sentiment. Here's why
items.summary: IT stocks such as Infosys and Oracle Financial Services Software gained, while LTIMindtree, Coforge, Tech Mahindra, Mphasis and Persistent Systems also posted strong gains. Meanwhile, the BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 dropped over 1% as the rupee hit a record low and bond yields surged to all-time highs.

items.title: New fuel-efficiency rules, global testing systems could raise car prices, lower mileage
items.summary: Starting April 1, 2027, the government will replace India’s Modified Indian Driving Cycle (MIDC) with a stricter global testing system and tighten fuel-efficiency norms under CAFE III regulations

items.title: Macro worries cloud markets, but domestic fundamentals offer cushion: Sandip Sabharwal
items.summary: Market expert Sandip Sabharwal highlights that despite global headwinds like rising crude oil and currency weakness, Indian corporate fundamentals offer stability. He views the Bharti-Prudential deal positively for FDI and sees potential tactical upside in the IT sector, while cautioning about Vodafone Idea's structural challenges and favoring private banks over PSUs.

items.summary: Asian Paints, SBI, Tata Motors, HUL and Maruti Suzuki are top gainers while ONGC, Axis Bank, MM, Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp are major losers in the Sensex.

items.title: Uber  redBus partner with ixigo for train travellers
items.summary: ixigo, India’s leading mobile travel marketplace has announced partnerships with Uber and redBus to enable instant bookings for cabs and buses natively within ixigo trains app.

items.title: Workload in IT to come down due to AI; jobs to reduce: Genpact
items.summary: But along with that the employment growth rates have started to dip, NV 'Tiger' Tyagarajan says. The percentage addition of employees in India will not be same as past. And due to advancements, a workforce with higher skill sets is required for the IT industry, he says.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.

items.title: Up over 200% in 1 year, what's buzzing with Borosil Glass?
items.summary: Shares of Borosil Glass Works have increased threefold over the past one year as the company has posted strong revenue and EBIDTA growth over the past few quarters.

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
